title: Rich Slovakian Pork Dumplings
description: A comforting Slovakian dish featuring pork, celery salt, and a unique mushimono-steam cooking method, low in sodium for a healthier option. This dish is perfect for a cold winter night.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine ground pork, flour, celery salt, and black pepper, mixing well with your hands until just combined.
In a small bowl, whisk together egg and milk, then add to the pork mixture, mixing until a sticky dough forms.
Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead for about 5 minutes, until the dough is smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each into a ball.
Place the dumplings in a steamer basket, leaving about 1 inch of space between each dumpling.
Steam the dumplings over boiling water for 15 minutes, or until cooked through.
While the dumplings are cooking, melt the butter in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Once the dumplings are done, remove them from the steamer and brush with the melted butter.
Serve the dumplings hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
